Client.title: Client
Client.description: Represents an individual or entity that receives loans from CrediControl.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Client entity.

identificationNumber.description: The client's national identification number (e.g., DNI, Passport, SSN).

notes.description: Additional notes or remarks about the client.

guaranteeIds.description: References to Guarantees associated with this client. (Relationship: Client 1:N Guarantee)

endorserIds.description: References to Endorsers who have endorsed this client's loans. (Relationship: Client N:N Endorser)

Guarantee.title: Guarantee
Guarantee.description: Represents an asset or collateral provided by a client to secure a loan.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Guarantee entity.

clientId.description: Reference to the Client who provided this guarantee. (Relationship: Client 1:N Guarantee)

description.description: A description of the guarantee (e.g., 'Car title', 'Property deed', 'Savings account').

value.description: The estimated monetary value of the guarantee.

documentationReference.description: A URI or reference to legal documentation for the guarantee (e.g., scanned deed, title document).

Endorser.title: Endorser
Endorser.description: Represents an individual who endorses a client's loan, providing additional security.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Endorser entity.

relationToClient.description: Describes the endorser's relationship to the client (e.g., 'Family member', 'Friend', 'Colleague').

endorsedClientIds.description: References to Clients whose loans this endorser has endorsed. (Relationship: Endorser N:N Client)

LoanType.title: LoanType
LoanType.description: Defines the characteristics and terms for different types of loan products offered.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LoanType entity.

name.description: A descriptive name for the loan type (e.g., 'Weekly 110/1000/12', 'Monthly Fixed Rate').

description.description: A detailed description of the loan type's terms and conditions (e.g., '110 weekly for every 1000 borrowed for 12 weeks').

principalAmountBasis.description: The base principal amount used for calculating payment terms (e.g., 1000 in '110 per 1000').

paymentAmountFactor.description: The payment amount associated with the principal basis (e.g., 110 in '110 per 1000').

numberOfPayments.description: The total number of payments required for the loan type.

totalInterestRate.description: The total interest rate for the loan type, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.15 for 15%). This can be implicit from factor/basis/payments.

isActive.description: Indicates whether this loan type is currently active and available for new loans.

Loan.title: Loan
Loan.description: Represents a specific loan instance granted to a client.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Loan entity.

clientId.description: Reference to the Client who received this loan. (Relationship: Client 1:N Loan)

loanTypeId.description: Reference to the LoanType that defines the terms of this loan. (Relationship: LoanType 1:N Loan)

principalAmount.description: The original principal amount of money borrowed by the client.

totalAmountDue.description: The total amount expected to be paid back, including principal and all interest/fees.

currentBalance.description: The current outstanding balance of the loan.

startDate.description: The date when the loan was issued.

expectedEndDate.description: The calculated date when the loan is expected to be fully paid off.

status.description: The current status of the loan (e.g., 'Active', 'Paid', 'Overdue', 'Defaulted', 'Cancelled').

nextPaymentDueDate.description: The due date for the next scheduled payment.

notes.description: Additional notes or remarks about the specific loan.

Payment.title: Payment
Payment.description: Records an individual payment made by a client towards a specific loan.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Payment entity.

loanId.description: Reference to the Loan this payment is associated with. (Relationship: Loan 1:N Payment)

paymentDate.description: The date and time when the payment was received.

amountPaid.description: The monetary amount of the payment.

paymentMethod.description: The method used for the payment (e.g., 'Cash', 'Bank Transfer', 'Credit Card', 'Online Payment').

transactionReference.description: A unique reference number for the transaction, if available.

notes.description: Any additional notes about the payment.

User.title: User
User.description: Represents an internal administrator or agent who uses the CrediControl application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for login and communication.

role.description: The role of the user within the application (e.g., 'Admin', 'Agent', 'Auditor'), defining their permissions.

isActive.description: Indicates whether the user's account is currently active.

lastLoginDate.description: The date and time of the user's last successful login.

NavigationSection.title: NavigationSection
NavigationSection.description: Defines a section or menu item available in the mobile application's navigation bar.

id.description: Unique identifier for the NavigationSection entity.

name.description: The display name of the navigation section (e.g., 'Clients List', 'New Loan', 'Reports').

path.description: The internal route or identifier that the navigation section links to within the application.

iconName.description: A symbolic name or reference to the icon asset used to represent this section in the UI.

displayOrder.description: A numerical value indicating the order in which this section should appear in the navigation bar.

description.description: A brief explanation of what this navigation section offers.

UserNavigationAccess.title: UserNavigationAccess
UserNavigationAccess.description: Manages which specific navigation sections a User (admin/agent) is allowed to access.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserNavigationAccess entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who is being granted access. (Relationship: User N:N NavigationSection)

navigationSectionId.description: Reference to the NavigationSection being accessed. (Relationship: User N:N NavigationSection)

definition.description: Top-level collection for internal CrediControl administrators and agents. Each document represents a user, where `userId` corresponds to `request.auth.uid`. The `role` field within this document is critical for database access control.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the CrediControl internal user, corresponding to Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Top-level collection defining global characteristics and terms for different loan products offered. Managed by administrators, accessible (read-only) by agents. No direct user ownership.

params.name: loanTypeId
params.description: The unique ID of the loan type.

definition.description: Top-level collection defining the available sections or menu items for the mobile application's navigation bar. Managed by administrators. These are the master definitions.

params.name: sectionId
params.description: The unique ID of the navigation section.

definition.description: Subcollection under a specific user, granting access to a particular navigation section. The document ID is the `navigationSectionId`. The document itself can be empty or contain audit fields (e.g., `grantedByUserId`, `timestamp`). This structure ensures authorization independence as `userId` is in the path and `navigationSectionId` is the document ID for direct lookup.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the CrediControl internal user.

params.name: navigationSectionId
params.description: The unique ID of the navigation section being granted or revoked.

definition.description: Top-level collection for all client records. Access is managed by internal CrediControl user roles (Admin, Agent). No direct client user authentication.

params.name: clientId
params.description: The unique ID of the client.

definition.description: Subcollection under a specific client, containing guarantees provided by that client. `clientId` is available in the path, ensuring authorization independence for security rules.

params.name: clientId
params.description: The unique ID of the client associated with the guarantee.

params.name: guaranteeId
params.description: The unique ID of the guarantee.

definition.description: Subcollection under a specific client, containing all loans issued to that client. `clientId` is available in the path, and `loanTypeId` is a field within the `Loan` document, ensuring authorization independence.

params.name: clientId
params.description: The unique ID of the client who received the loan.

params.name: loanId
params.description: The unique ID of the loan.

definition.description: Subcollection under a specific loan, containing individual payments made towards that loan. `clientId` and `loanId` are available in the path, ensuring authorization independence.

params.name: clientId
params.description: The unique ID of the client associated with the loan.

params.name: loanId
params.description: The unique ID of the loan for which the payment was made.

params.name: paymentId
params.description: The unique ID of the payment.

definition.description: Top-level collection for endorser records. Endorsers can be associated with multiple clients, and this flat structure reflects the N:N relationship. `endorsedClientIds` field facilitates querying. Access is managed by internal CrediControl user roles.

params.name: endorserId
params.description: The unique ID of the endorser.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for CrediControl prioritizes Authorization Independence and Structural Segregation to ensure robust security rules and efficient data access. The design employs a mix of top-level collections for core business entities and user-specific configurations, along with hierarchical subcollections for deeply related data, following a `User 1:N Entity` relationship pattern where applicable. The central principle for authorization is Database Access Control (DBAC) using the `User` entity's `role` field, which will be fetched for `request.auth.uid` to determine permissions across various collections. This avoids `get()` calls to parent documents for authorization of child documents.

**Authorization Independence:**
*   For `User` related data, such as `UserNavigationAccess`, a hierarchical path `/users/{userId}/navigationAccesses/{navigationSectionId}` is used. Here, the `userId` is directly available in the path, allowing rules to check `request.auth.uid == userId` without needing to `get()` the parent `User` document to verify ownership. The `navigationSectionId` is used as the document ID for `UserNavigationAccess`, making it readily available in the path as well.
*   For core business entities like `Client`, `Loan`, `Guarantee`, `Endorser`, and `LoanType`, access is primarily determined by the `role` of the authenticated CrediControl internal user (`request.auth.uid`). These entities are mostly housed in top-level collections, or in subcollections where the parent's `id` (e.g., `clientId`, `loanId`) is available in the path. This means that authorization rules for these collections will directly check the `request.auth.uid`'s role (e.g., `isAdmin()` or `isAgent()`, where `isAdmin()` is a helper function that performs a single `get()` on the current user's `/users/{uid}` document to check their role). No `get()` calls are made to other data documents to determine access. For instance, a `Payment` document under `/clients/{clientId}/loans/{loanId}/payments/{paymentId}` inherently includes `clientId` and `loanId` in its path, so authorization for `Payment` does not require getting the parent `Loan` or `Client` documents.

**QAPs (Queries as Permissions):**
*   **User-Specific Data:** The structure `/users/{userId}/navigationAccesses/{navigationSectionId}` directly supports QAPs. A user can easily `list` their specific navigation permissions by querying `db.collection('users').doc(request.auth.uid).collection('navigationAccesses')`. Security rules can then enforce `allow list: if request.auth.uid == userId;` ensuring users only retrieve their own assigned sections.
*   **Role-Based Data:** For entities like `Client`, `LoanType`, `Loan`, `Payment`, `Guarantee`, and `Endorser`, access is role-based. For example, `allow list: if isAdmin() || isAgent();`. This means a `list` query will return all documents in the collection that are visible to that role. If fine-grained access (e.g., agents only see *their* clients) were required, the `Client` documents would need a denormalized `assignedAgentId` field, allowing a `list` rule like `allow list: if isAdmin() || (isAgent() && request.query.where.assignedAgentId == request.auth.uid);`. Based on the current request, this level of client-agent assignment is not specified, so a general role-based access for `list` operations is assumed for internal users.
